company.name: Guardio
company.notes: Security but has an AI Creative Specialist role — non-obvious lead.

research.status_summary: Guardio is actively hiring across multiple roles (24 open jobs as of mid-2026) with no layoffs reported, riding post-Series-B growth momentum following its $80M raise in November 2025.
research.hiring_policy: All open roles are Israel-based (Tel Aviv HQ); roughly 71% of listed jobs carry some remote tag but the company recruits talent for Israel and does not publicly advertise visa sponsorship or EOR arrangements — no evidence of international sponsorship found in any job listing.
recent_news: November 2025: Closed $80M Series B led by ION Crossover Partners (valuation tripled vs. 2021 Tiger Global round), targeting $100M ARR as early as 2026 after three consecutive years of 100%+ ARR growth.

Late 2025 / Jan 2026: Partnered with vibe-coding unicorn Lovable to embed Guardio's Safe Browsing detection engine into Lovable's GenAI chain, scanning every AI-generated site at the moment of creation — Guardio's first B2B deal and a marquee signal of its pivot toward AI-generated threat detection.

Headcount grew to ~144 employees (Tracxn, Feb 2026) with new senior hires including a Director of Data and a CMO; ZoomInfo signals more HR and IT roles open now than at any point in the past 12 months.
research.growth_signal: Headcount at 144 (Feb 2026, up from ~127 mid-2025), $127M total funding, $100M ARR target for 2026, and a tripled valuation since 2021 all point to a company in aggressive but disciplined scale-up mode.
research.fit_for_lior: The AI Creative Specialist role sits at the exact intersection of Lior's threads — Guardio is a consumer-brand cybersecurity company that explicitly blends 'world-class engineering with bold creativity,' is deep in the AI-generated-threat space (Lovable partnership), and needs creative AI execution to drive its US brand expansion post-Series-B.
